title: ARRY Q1 2026 Deep Dive: International Expansion and New Product Launches
text: Array Technologies has well-embedded home bases in legacy markets in Spain and Brazil, historically some of largest in world, but now facing macro challenges with curtailments and other factors slowing growth. International markets, notably parts of Brazil and Spain, facing macro headwinds such as power curtailment issues and aggressive low-cost competition, creating uncertainty around project timing. Rather than relying primarily on Brazil and Spain, Array is adopting selective approach to international expansion. Company has been very open about being selective and returns-focused in where it strives to diversify internationally, looking to locations that specifically have appreciation for value proposition for difficult terrain, potentially extreme weather, and areas where domestic content is requirement. Company has signed contracts in markets such as Turkey, Peru, and Colombia as it builds more diversified international platform. Additionally, company launched DuraTrack D2S dual-row tracker, which saw first commercial deployment in Spain and received positive early feedback. While Spain and Brazil remain important legacy markets for Array, company is pursuing targeted strategy focused on geographic diversification into regions where technology offers competitive advantages for challenging terrain and extreme weather conditions.
